mnemonics: PMF: Points Make Frequency (discrete), PDF: Picture of Density Function (continuous)
Expectation is a Weighted Average: Σ x * P(x) or ∫ x * f(x) dx
keyPoints: A PMF (Probability Mass Function) gives the probability that a discrete random variable takes on a specific value.
A PDF (Probability Density Function) describes the relative likelihood for a continuous random variable to take on a given value; probability is found by integrating over an interval.
Mathematical Expectation (or Expected Value) is the long-run average value of a random variable, calculated as a weighted sum (discrete) or integral (continuous) of all possible values.
commonMistakes: Evaluating a PDF at a single point and interpreting it as a probability (for continuous variables, P(X=x)=0).
Confusing the properties of PMFs and PDFs (e.g., applying the sum-to-1 rule of a PMF to a PDF, which must integrate to 1).
Forgetting that expectation is a linear operator: E[aX + b] = aE[X] + b, but this is not generally true for variance or other moments.
